hinojosa-gina.name: Gina Hinojosa
hinojosa-gina.background: Gina Hinojosa, state representative from the 49th district (2017–present).

dixon-pat.name: Pat Dixon
dixon-pat.background: Pat Dixon (Libertarian), former chair of the Libertarian Party of Texas

goodwin-vikki.name: Vikki Goodwin
goodwin-vikki.background: Vikki Goodwin, state representative from the 47th district (2019–present).

collier-mike.name: Mike Collier
collier-mike.background: Mike Collier (Independent), certified public accountant, Democratic nominee for Lt. Governor in 2018 and 2022, Democratic nominee for Comptroller in 2014

cristo-anthony.name: Anthony Cristo
cristo-anthony.background: Anthony Cristo (Libertarian), nominee for Texas's 15th congressional district in 2018, and nominee for Texas's 34th congressional district in 2020

middleton-mayes.name: Mayes Middleton
middleton-mayes.background: Mayes Middleton, state senator from the 11th district (2023–present).

johnson-nathan.name: Nathan Johnson
johnson-nathan.background: Nathan Johnson, state senator from the 16th district (2019–present).

oxford-tom.name: Tom Oxford
oxford-tom.background: Tom Oxford (Libertarian), attorney and perennial candidate

paxton-ken.name: Ken Paxton
paxton-ken.background: Ken Paxton, attorney general of Texas (2015–present).

talarico-james.name: James Talarico
talarico-james.background: James Talarico, state representative from the 50th district (2018–present).

huffines-don.name: Don Huffines
huffines-don.background: Don Huffines, former state senator from the 16th district (2015–2019) and candidate for governor in 2022.

eckhardt-sarah.name: Sarah Eckhardt
eckhardt-sarah.background: Sarah Eckhardt, state senator from the 14th district (2020–present).

flores-benjamin.name: Benjamin Flores
flores-benjamin.background: Benjamin Flores, Bay City councilor (previously ran for governor).

sheets-nate.name: Nate Sheets
sheets-nate.background: Nate Sheets, beekeeper and small business owner.

tucker-clayton.name: Clayton Tucker
tucker-clayton.background: Clayton Tucker, rancher and founder of the Texas Progressive Caucus.

french-bo.name: Bo French
french-bo.background: Bo French, former chair of the Tarrant County Republican Party.

rosenthal-jon.name: Jon Rosenthal
rosenthal-jon.background: Jon Rosenthal, state representative from the 135th district (2019–present).

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote barred the President from declaring or enforcing a moratorium on hydraulic fracturing (fracking) for oil and gas without prior congressional approval.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the 2025 budget reconciliation package extending the 2017 tax cuts, adding new tax breaks (e.g. on tips and overtime pay), cutting projected Medicaid and SNAP spending, boosting border and defense funding, and raising the debt limit.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed a Republican-backed alternative to extending the enhanced ACA marketplace subsidies, restructuring health-insurance premium subsidy rules for individual-market plans.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote barred state Medicaid programs from using federal Medicaid funds to cover gender-transition procedures for minors.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026, funding most of the federal government for the fiscal year and averting a shutdown.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Homeowner Energy Freedom Act, rolling back certain federal energy-efficiency and appliance/equipment regulations that affect homeowners.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, funding DHS (including CBP and ICE border security and immigration enforcement operations) for the fiscal year.

key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Homeowner Energy Freedom Act, rolling back certain federal energy-efficiency and appliance/equipment regulations that affect homeowners.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, funding DHS (including CBP and ICE border security and immigration enforcement operations) for the fiscal year.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote barred the President from declaring or enforcing a moratorium on hydraulic fracturing (fracking) for oil and gas without prior congressional approval.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the 2025 budget reconciliation package extending the 2017 tax cuts, adding new tax breaks (e.g. on tips and overtime pay), cutting projected Medicaid and SNAP spending, boosting border and defense funding, and raising the debt limit.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed a Republican-backed alternative to extending the enhanced ACA marketplace subsidies, restructuring health-insurance premium subsidy rules for individual-market plans.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote barred state Medicaid programs from using federal Medicaid funds to cover gender-transition procedures for minors.

key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026, funding most of the federal government for the fiscal year and averting a shutdown.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Homeowner Energy Freedom Act, rolling back certain federal energy-efficiency and appliance/equipment regulations that affect homeowners.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2026, funding DHS (including CBP and ICE border security and immigration enforcement operations) for the fiscal year.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote barred the President from declaring or enforcing a moratorium on hydraulic fracturing (fracking) for oil and gas without prior congressional approval.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed the 2025 budget reconciliation package extending the 2017 tax cuts, adding new tax breaks (e.g. on tips and overtime pay), cutting projected Medicaid and SNAP spending, boosting border and defense funding, and raising the debt limit.

key_votes.question: On Passage
key_votes.plain_english: A Yea vote passed a Republican-backed alternative to extending the enhanced ACA marketplace subsidies, restructuring health-insurance premium subsidy rules for individual-market plans.
